title: EnvironmentFile does not honor "-" prefix from systemd.exec
body:
# Problem

Aligning with the behavior of systemd, the EnvironmentFile (and possible other options) should behave like their counterparts in systemd.exec 

## Definition of key 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/291b97f1e84626bb77e3b2ab53d9c91b083f26d0/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L84

## Relevant passage from [systemd.exec](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.exec.html) man page:

> EnvironmentFile=
>
> Similar to Environment=, but reads the environment variables from a text file. The text file should contain newline-separated variable assignments. Empty lines, lines without an "=" separator, or lines starting with ";" or "#" will be ignored, which may be used for commenting. The file must be encoded with UTF-8. Valid characters are [unicode scalar values](https://www.unicode.org/glossary/#unicode_scalar_value) other than [unicode noncharacters](https://www.unicode.org/glossary/#noncharacter), U+0000 NUL, and U+FEFF [unicode byte order mark](https://www.unicode.org/glossary/#byte_order_mark). Control codes other than NUL are allowed.
> 
> In the file, an unquoted value after the "=" is parsed with the same backslash-escape rules as [POSIX shell unquoted text](https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/utilities/V3_chap02.html#tag_18_02_01), but unlike in a shell, interior whitespace is preserved and quotes after the first non-whitespace character are preserved. Leading and trailing whitespace (space, tab, carriage return) is discarded, but interior whitespace within the line is preserved verbatim. A line ending with a backslash will be continued to the following one, with the newline itself discarded. A backslash "\" followed by any character other than newline will preserve the following character, so that "\\" will become the value "\".
> 
> In the file, a "'"-quoted value after the "=" can span multiple lines and contain any character verbatim other than single quote, like [POSIX shell single-quoted text](https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/utilities/V3_chap02.html#tag_18_02_02). No backslash-escape sequences are recognized. Leading and trailing whitespace outside of the single quotes is discarded.
> 
> In the file, a """-quoted value after the "=" can span multiple lines, and the same escape sequences are recognized as in [POSIX shell double-quoted text](https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/utilities/V3_chap02.html#tag_18_02_03). Backslash ("\") followed by any of ""\`$" will preserve that character. A backslash followed by newline is a line continuation, and the newline itself is discarded. A backslash followed by any other character is ignored; both the backslash and the following character are preserved verbatim. Leading and trailing whitespace outside of the double quotes is discarded.
> 
> The argument passed should be an absolute filename or wildcard expression, **optionally prefixed with "-", which indicates that if the file does not exist, it will not be read and no error or warning message is logged.** This option may be specified more than once in which case all specified files are read. If the empty string is assigned to this option, the list of file to read is reset, all prior assignments have no effect.
> 
> The files listed with this directive will be read shortly before the process is executed (more specifically, after all processes from a previous unit state terminated. This means you can generate these files in one unit state, and read it with this option in the next. The files are read from the file system of the service manager, before any file system changes like bind mounts take place).
> 
> Settings from these files override settings made with Environment=. If the same variable is set twice from these files, the files will be read in the order they are specified and the later setting will override the earlier setting.

## Example of failure 

Here is an example using the directive `EnvironmentFile=/srv/containers/qemu/runner-%i/conf/qemu.env` (with and without the prefix):

```diff
Jul 29 18:43:23 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reload requested from client PID 1906597 ('systemctl')...
Jul 29 18:43:23 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reloading...
Jul 29 18:43:24 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1906601]: quadlet-generator[1906601]: Warning: qemu-runner@.container specifies the image "qemux/qemu" which not a fully qualified image name. This is not ideal for performance and security reasons. See the podman-pull manpage discussion of short-name-aliases.conf for details.
Jul 29 18:43:24 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reloading finished in 103 ms.
Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Starting qemu-runner@8006.service - QEMU Runner Instance 8006...
-Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel qemu-runner@8006[1906615]: Error: parsing file "/srv/containers/qemu/runner-8006/conf/qemu.env": open /srv/containers/qemu/runner-8006/conf/qemu.env: no such file or directory
Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel podman[1906615]: 2025-07-29 18:43:28.721376207 -0700 PDT m=+0.016823600 image pull 24c9e28fb98b9ae6937b2ef4e5bc5890b60912855048cbb37cbad90655da1efb qemux/qemu
Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: qemu-runner@8006.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: qemu-runner@8006.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 29 18:43:28 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Failed to start qemu-runner@8006.service - QEMU Runner Instance 8006.
Jul 29 18:44:18 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reload requested from client PID 1906676 ('systemctl')...
Jul 29 18:44:18 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reloading...
Jul 29 18:44:18 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1906680]: quadlet-generator[1906680]: Warning: qemu-runner@.container specifies the image "qemux/qemu" which not a fully qualified image name. This is not ideal for performance and security reasons. See the podman-pull manpage discussion of short-name-aliases.conf for details.
Jul 29 18:44:18 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Reloading finished in 112 ms.
Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Starting qemu-runner@8006.service - QEMU Runner Instance 8006...
-Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel qemu-runner@8006[1906694]: Error: parsing file "/home/podman/.config/containers/systemd/-/srv/containers/qemu/runner-8006/conf/qemu.env": open /home/podman/.config/containers/systemd/-/srv/containers/qemu/runner-8006/conf/qemu.env: no such file or directory
Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel podman[1906694]: 2025-07-29 18:44:21.481497031 -0700 PDT m=+0.015404349 image pull 24c9e28fb98b9ae6937b2ef4e5bc5890b60912855048cbb37cbad90655da1efb qemux/qemu
Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: qemu-runner@8006.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: qemu-runner@8006.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 29 18:44:21 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Failed to start qemu-runner@8006.service - QEMU Runner Instance 8006.
Jul 29 18:52:36 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Starting systemd-tmpfiles-clean.service - Cleanup of User's Temporary Files and Directories...
Jul 29 18:52:36 berstuk.los2.kafka.tel systemd[1676]: Finished systemd-tmpfiles-clean.service - Cleanup of User's Temporary Files and Directories.

```

comments.body: Concern: is this a breaking change, given that (right now) I can do `--env-file -my-file` and match `-my-file`, but after this change it will match `my-file`

> and match -my-file

Yeah agree that is a breaking change technically even if we think such filenames are super unlikely.

Also adding `-` support is one thing but the quoted docs describe and entirely different format as well as far as the content goes and we certainly cannot chnage podman --env-file format without breaking people.


FWIW there was already this option added for --device in quadlet in d51911f7b0ee320c7f35410dd0ae2add75908a7f.
I think doing this in quadlet is generally wrong though because quadlet checks at the time the generator runs and not at the time the podman command actually gets executed.

> I think doing this in quadlet is generally wrong though because quadlet checks at the time the generator runs and not at the time the podman command actually gets executed.

Agree. This means that users will either have to make sure the files exist before calling `daemon-reload` or have to run it again after placing the files.

So, maybe this should be a feature request from Podman
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Maybe it makes sense to add the `-` syntax to the various options that accept a file path as part of 6.0 where we can make this as breaking change?
--env-file, --device, --volume, --label-file, --authfile and more?

As for a different env-file format itself that almost certainly requires a new option as breaking people there seem much much more likely, see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18724
